name: Nacelle Headless Commerce API
what_it_does: Headless commerce data layer and storefront platform with GraphQL API — abstracting product, catalog, and cart data from multiple commerce backends (Shopify, BigCommerce, Salesforce Commerce Cloud) into a unified GraphQL API, enabling high-performance headless storefronts with pre-built React components and caching.
use_cases: Building high-performance headless storefronts consuming Nacelle unified GraphQL API
Syncing product catalog data from Shopify or BigCommerce via Nacelle's indexing layer
Querying unified product, collection, and content data for Next.js or Nuxt storefronts
Managing cart sessions and order initiation via Nacelle's composable cart API
Integrating Nacelle product data with search engines (Algolia, Constructor) for fast discovery
not_for: Merchants without headless storefront requirements (use Shopify directly)
Platforms needing real-time inventory and order management (Nacelle is catalog/UX layer)
Simple single-channel stores without performance or composability requirements
best_when: A DTC brand or enterprise retailer running Shopify Plus or BigCommerce wants a high-performance headless storefront with composable architecture, pre-built components, and a unified data API layer.
avoid_when: You're happy with standard Shopify or BigCommerce themes, don't have developer resources for headless, or aren't willing to maintain a headless frontend.

auth.notes: Nacelle space ID and token from dashboard. Passed in GraphQL request headers. Space-scoped access (space = Nacelle project). Read-only storefront token for frontend. Write token for backend/admin operations. No OAuth2. Webhook for catalog sync events.

pricing.notes: Los Angeles-based company. Founded 2019. Raised $30M. Commerce-focused PaaS. Storefront SDK with pre-built React components. Automatic product catalog indexing from Shopify/BigCommerce. Edge caching for storefront performance. Integrations with Contentful, Sanity, Algolia. Strong DTC and enterprise retail adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API (not REST) for products, collections, content, and cart. Space ID and token in headers. Good documentation with GraphQL schema explorer. Separate read/write tokens with different permissions. Catalog data is indexed — sync latency from commerce backend. JavaScript SDK for React integrations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All product and collection reads fully idempotent. Cart operations idempotent by session ID. Content queries idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL only — no REST API for storefront data
Space ID required alongside API token in headers
Catalog data is indexed (not real-time) — sync delay from Shopify/BigCommerce source
Separate read and write tokens — don't expose write token in frontend code
Cart API delegates to commerce backend (Shopify) — understand checkout URL handoff

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. Read/write token separation for frontend/backend. Edge-cached storefront data has excellent performance. Write token must never be exposed in frontend. Product pricing data in API — implement appropriate access controls.
